verdicts.ATTACKER_CONTINUITY_rationale: Fixed tests pass and the structural deck-blindness it targets is real and prevalent, but the candidate does NOT improve the win rate. n=120 looked positive (mirror +4.2) but the n=240 escalation REFUTED it: mirror 47.1% (49.4% pooled = even), alakazam -3.7, denpa92 -0.8. The n=120 gain was noise; the offline mechanism signal was already weak. Keep the term DISABLED (documented dead-end); the deck-blind FINDING is still valid input for Model A's learned approach, but the hand-weighted leaf term is not promotable.
verdicts.TACTICAL_STATE_EXPORT_rationale: 22,083 rows, every observation resolved, runtime/eval separation leak-checked, feature taxonomy documented (deck-independent vs Starmie-role vs card-id vs public-runtime vs eval-only).

submission_decision: NO SUBMISSION. Nothing materially improved. main is unchanged == sub_starmie2 already on the ladder. BOTH candidate fixes this session were refuted by proper A/B (attack-floor heuristic regressed everything; ATTACKER_CONTINUITY leaf neutral/slightly-negative at n=240). The diagnoses were real but neither fix won. Lesson: n~120 local A/B is noise-dominated; local intuitions keep failing to beat the agent's own search judgment.
stop_conditions_respected: no Model A research files modified
